title: RSC Internals and Relationship to SSR

learningObjectives.text: Explain rsc rendering protocol using a new example and justify the result.
Explain rsc payload using a new example and justify the result.
Explain how rsc and ssr differ using a new example and justify the result.
Explain how they work together using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in rsc internals and relationship to ssr recur throughout later Next.js work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **React Server Components** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **RSC rendering protocol** → **RSC payload** → **How RSC and SSR differ** → **How they work together**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: RSC rendering protocol
sections.body: React Server Components serialize server-rendered results, client-component references, and props into an RSC payload.

sections.title: RSC payload
sections.body: The RSC payload describes the rendered server tree and client boundaries; it is not ordinary HTML.

sections.title: How RSC and SSR differ
sections.body: RSC chooses execution environments for component code; SSR is a technique that produces HTML on the server.

sections.title: How they work together
sections.body: Next.js can use RSC to assemble the tree and SSR to emit initial HTML, then hydrate client boundaries in the browser.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The server renders data and a reference to a Client Component in the RSC result.

commonMistakes: Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.
Confusing React Server Components with server-side rendering.
Assuming cached data is fresh without an explicit policy and invalidation path.
Placing security-sensitive validation only in the client or middleware layer.
summary: RSC Internals and Relationship to SSR connects RSC rendering protocol, RSC payload, How RSC and SSR differ.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

assessmentProfile.label: Conceptual and mental-model review
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic is primarily definitions, mental models, comparisons, or framework purpose. Scenario MCQs are more appropriate than forced code trivia.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
